implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: Clean, minimal implementation that satisfies both acceptance criteria. Four lines added to one file — exactly the right scope. The footer is placed inside <main> rather than as a sibling, which is a minor semantic HTML nit but matches the spec's explicit technical approach of adding to page.tsx. Dynamic year and 'BrewBoard' copyright text are correct.

requirements.notes: Both acceptance criteria met: footer at bottom of page, copyright text with dynamic year and 'BrewBoard'. Spec says 'every page' in the summary but the technical approach specifically targets page.tsx — implementation follows the technical approach. Minor: footer is inside <main> rather than a layout-level element, but spec doesn't require otherwise.

correctness.notes: new Date().getFullYear() is correct JSX for dynamic year rendering. Tailwind classes are valid. The © character renders correctly in JSX. Footer is properly nested in the component tree.

minimality.notes: 4 insertions, 1 file changed. No tests, no docs, no refactoring — exactly as the out-of-scope section requires. Textbook minimal change.

code_quality.notes: Clean Tailwind utility classes with sensible styling choices (border-t separator, muted text color, centered). Semantic <footer> element. Slight ding for placing <footer> inside <main> — conventionally it sits outside — but consistent with the single-component structure of the page.

risk.notes: No regressions possible from an additive 4-line change. <footer> landmark inside <main> is mildly non-ideal for screen reader landmark navigation, but negligible risk for this app. No layout shift or styling conflicts expected.
